summary: Crystal‑clear soundboard capture of the legendary September ’74 “Eyes of the World” suite with a searing Seastones intermission and a mini‑Dark Star transition.
review: The partial SBD delivers an outstanding performance – Ned’s electric‑piano lead into a flawless Eyes of the World, a gorgeous segue into Wharf Rat that feels like a miniature Dark Star, and the rare Seastones jam that pushes the band to outer‑space heights. The mix is pristine, rivaling the best 1974 soundboard recordings, making this an essential listen for any Deadhead.

ai_rating.rationale: All ten reviews praise both the musical performance (multiple standout songs and unique jams) and the recording quality (crisp, clean soundboard), yielding a near‑perfect combined score.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp, clean mix with minimal hiss; cleaned‑up chunk noted by reviewers; consistently described as one of the best ’74 SBDs.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The set showcases a unique flow – Phil/Ned’s “Knee‑Trembler” leads into Eyes of the World, which transitions seamlessly into Wharf Rat (mini‑Dark Star), followed by an adventurous Seastones intermission and classic rockers like Around and Around.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for perfect tone, smooth pocket with Ned, subtle string‑bending that night, and big laughs after Wharf Rat.
band_member_comments.Phil: Mentioned as driving the jam forward; his bass work underpins the extended improvisations.
band_member_comments.Bob: Noted as part of the rhythm drive alongside Phil and Billy.
band_member_comments.Keys: Ned’s shriller electric‑piano lead highlighted (compared to Brent), with Keith supplying deeper grand piano fills; keyboards praised for symmetry and icing on the cake.
band_member_comments.Drums: Billy Kreutzmann joins the intermission jam, contributing to the “primo ’74 chaos” and overall propulsion.
